blocks.text: Statement of Work
OPTBOT Atlanta Office Refresh - Three Site Modernization

This fictional SOW defines a multi-site office refresh for OPTBOT in the Atlanta region. It is intentionally detailed so document parsers can extract scope, phase, site, commercial, acceptance, and risk information from a realistic Word file.

OPTBOT wants a consistent hybrid-work experience across headquarters, operations, and logistics environments. The implementation covers AV room standards, network edge upgrades, desk hardware, rugged warehouse devices, scheduling panels, staging, installation, training, and hypercare.

items.text: Stakeholder interview notes
Existing-room matrix
Network closet readiness checklist
Final room standards and deployment runbook
Hardware order tracking
Asset labels and serial capture
Image validation for rugged tablets
Site-specific install kits
After-hours installation waves
Room device commissioning
Wi-Fi AP swap and validation
Desk accessory deployment
Punch-list tracking
Admin training
Facilities handoff
Help desk quick reference
Final asset inventory
Hypercare report
OPTBOT provides site access, escorts, loading dock windows, and after-hours approvals at least five business days before each installation wave.
All mock contact details, order numbers, and addresses in this packet are fictional and must not be copied to production systems.
Customer network team confirms VLAN, DHCP, DNS, firewall, and certificate requirements before staging begins.
Hardware substitutions require written approval from the technical buyer and update to the bill of materials workbook.
The dev integration team owns HubSpot attachment upload, Azure copy validation, parser-os execution, and OrbitBrief brief generation.
New construction, electrical trenching, permanent conduit installation, furniture procurement, production billing, legal review, and real customer communications are out of scope.
Production tenant access, real OPTBOT credentials, and real payment processing are explicitly excluded.

This fictional procurement packet supports OPTBOT Atlanta Office Refresh - Three Site Modernization. It references quote Q-DEV-ATL-047-R3, mock PO PO-MOCK-77421, and mock MSA
The packet is designed to test parser recognition of approvals, payment terms, procurement controls, line-item rationale, signature blocks, and checklist actions.

Current rooms use inconsistent camera, microphone, calendar, and scheduling-panel setups, causing meeting delays and uneven user experience. Desk-accessory standards vary by floor and site, creating support complexity and asset tracking gaps. The refresh standardizes collaboration spaces, improves wireless and warehouse device reliability, upgrades network closets, and creates a cleaner asset baseline for future service operations.

Total mock deal amount: $1,847,250.00. CFO approval required over $1,500,000. Budget owner approval required over $250,000. Jordan Ames approves workplace outcome and business case. Priya Narang approves technical design. Camila Brooks approves security and data handling. Elliot Tran approves procurement release. Renee Watkins accepts delivery governance artifacts.

Quote valid through 2026-06-14. Hardware pricing assumes consolidated shipment to Atlanta staging facility. Substitutions require written approval from Priya Narang and an updated BOM workbook. Payment schedule: 30% order acceptance, 40% equipment receipt, 20% site acceptance, 10% hypercare closeout. Change orders are required for new sites, material quantity changes, accelerated shipping, or labor above 1,680 after-hours hours. No real legal obligations are created by this document. All clauses are mock-only and should not be used as customer-facing language.

Confirm HubSpot dev deal exists and is owned by dev-integration-owner@example.com. Attach exactly seven packet files and verify filenames preserve sequence numbers. Copy every attachment to Azure with mock=true and dealId metadata. Run parser-os and confirm extraction of total amount, sites, stakeholders, payment schedule, approval matrix, and risks. Run OrbitBrief and confirm the brief states that the data is fictional and references ATL- HQ, ATL-WEST, and ATL-AIR. Record mock approval notes from Jordan Ames, Priya Narang, Camila Brooks, Elliot Tran, and CFO delegate Morgan Lee.

Jordan Ames: Approved business case pending final cutover calendar. Priya Narang: Approved technical design pending ATL-WEST carrier confirmation. Camila Brooks: Approved dev-only handling with production-blocking controls. Elliot Tran: Procurement can issue PO-MOCK-77421 after CFO threshold approval. and payment schedule.

Allowed destinations: HubSpot dev, Azure dev blob storage, parser-os dev workers, OrbitBrief dev workspace. Blocked destinations: production CRM, production Azure tenants, customer email, billing systems, and public repositories.

HubSpot Dev Deal -> attachment upload -> Azure copy job -> parser-os extraction -> extracted JSON/table output -> OrbitBrief brief generation -> HubSpot dev timeline note. Canonical key for every step: HS-DEAL-OPTBOT-ATL-2026-047. Azure storage path: mock-hubspot- dev/opbtot-atl-refresh-2026-047.

DOCX extraction should preserve SOW sections, site survey tables, assumptions, exclusions, acceptance criteria, and requirements. XLSX extraction should preserve workbook names, sheet names, formulas, totals, site allocations, task dependencies, risk register rows, and integration checkpoints. Cross-document reconciliation should flag any mismatch in deal ID, total amount, site count, close date, or quote number.

Brief title should mention OPTBOT Atlanta Office Refresh. Summary should mention all three sites and the mock-only classification. Key risks should include ATL-WEST circuit timing, ATL-HQ blackout, ATL-AIR RF reliability, and CFO approval threshold.

Recommended next actions should include upload validation, Azure metadata check, parser extraction baseline, OrbitBrief summary review, and procurement approval. Use fictional Intune profile INTUNE-MOCK-OPTBOT-ATL-REFRESH for rugged tablet references.
